Case Studies
64 chars

ESPC Case Study: How South Euclid Got $6.2M in Upgrades for Free

Description
South Euclid used an Energy Savings Performance Contract to modernize $6.2 million in city facilities — paid entirely by guaranteed energy savings. Zero cost to taxpayers. The buildings were aging. Maintenance deferred for years. No budget to fix them. Leopardo Efficiency evaluated their operations, identified the savings, and financed 100% of the project. The savings cover the debt service. Residents pay nothing extra. Buildings good for another 40 years. This is the ESPC model. South Euclid is the proof. leopardoefficiency.com
Tags
ESPC · energy savings performance contract · South Euclid Ohio · zero taxpayer cost
02
How It Works
67 chars

What Is an ESPC? How Cities Fund Infrastructure With Energy Savings

Description
Energy Savings Performance Contracts let cities modernize infrastructure without tax increases, bond referendums, or upfront capital — and the savings are guaranteed in writing. Most cities have more capital needs than capital. Roofs, HVAC, windows, boilers — deferred for years because there's no budget. An ESPC changes that. Leopardo Efficiency evaluates your operations, identifies where you're losing money, and finances 100% of the project. The energy savings pay for the upgrades. This video walks through exactly how the mechanism works. leopardoefficiency.com
Tags
ESPC explained · energy savings performance contract · municipal infrastructure financing · no upfront cost
03
Case Studies
65 chars

Speedway Indiana: Turning Wastewater Into $120K in Annual Savings

Description
Municipal energy savings from an unexpected source — Speedway, Indiana captured methane gas from their wastewater treatment process and turned it into $80,000–$120,000 in annual operational savings. Utility rates were climbing. Instead of raising sewer bills, they looked for another way. Leopardo Efficiency identified the opportunity: a self-funding project that reduces reliance on outside electricity and gas — using a resource they were previously burning off. Rates held. Residents protected. "I think that's something all municipalities should look at — particularly those with water or wastewater treatment plants." leopardoefficiency.com
Tags
wastewater treatment energy savings · municipal utility savings · methane gas capture · Speedway Indiana
04
Case Studies
64 chars

Cleveland Modernized 3 Police Districts — Here's How It Was Done

Description
City building modernization across Cleveland's Districts 1, 4, and 5 — new boilers, full HVAC replacement, exterior upgrades, all done with local union labor. A promise was made to the City of Cleveland. New boiler systems installed mid-winter, carrying buildings through the full heating season. Chillers, air handling, pumps, piping, and control systems operational by June. Roofing, masonry, concrete, and sod restoration completed. Every bit of it done the right way — local coordination, local workers, real work inside Cleveland neighborhoods. leopardoefficiency.com
Tags
Cleveland infrastructure · police station renovation · municipal building modernization · local union labor

Case Studies
62 chars

South Euclid Ribbon Cut: $6.2M in Upgrades, Zero Taxpayer Cost

Description
Zero taxpayer cost. $6.2 million in city facility upgrades. Fully financed by guaranteed energy savings — and a community that came together to get it done. Financially responsible. Environmentally friendly. Local job creation. Over $300,000 in additional income generated through local union labor. No tax increase. No bond referendum. No check written by residents. "Because of you, we can come together and thrive." leopardoefficiency.com
Tags
South Euclid Ohio · ribbon cutting · ESPC project completion · zero taxpayer cost · local union labor
